structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages shaders for OpenGL rendering. The class has methods for binding the shader program, uniform floats, and matrix 4f uniforms. It also includes utility functions for loading shader text from files and validating the shader program after compilation.

items.description: in Java provides functionality for binding a program and uniform values, as well as loading shaders from files. The class also includes methods for validating the program and getting location of uniforms and matrices.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glues a program to a context, enabling its use for rendering.
items.name: bind

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `program`.

usage.code:
int program = shader.getProgram();  // get the ID of the shader program
GL20.glUseProgram(program);        // use the shader program for rendering

items.name: getProgram

items.description: takes a string name and an array of floats as input, and uses the `glUniformf` method to set the corresponding uniform value in a GL context based on the length of the input array.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location for which the values are being set.

items.name: uniformf

items.description: updates a uniform matrix with the specified name using the `glUniformMatrix4` method from the `GL20` class, passing in the matrix's linear data in a flipped buffer.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location where the matrix will be stored.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 matrix of homogeneous coordinates that is to be set as a uniform buffer object for rendering purposes using the `GL20.glUniformMatrix4()` method.

* `name`: The name of the uniform location where the matrix will be stored.
* `mat`: A `Matrix4f` object representing a 4x4 matrix with floating-point elements. Its linear data is stored in a buffer that is created using `Util.createFlippedBuffer()`.
* `getLocation(name)`: A method of the current GL20 context that returns a handle to a specific uniform location, which is specified by the `name` parameter. The location is retrieved using the `GL20.glGetUniformLocation()` function and stored in a member variable for later use.
* `false`: A boolean value indicating whether the matrix should be transferred from the Vertex Shader or the Fragment Shader. In this case, it is transferred from the Vertex Shader.

usage.description: In this example, a Matrix4f named "mat" is created and initialized with some values. Then, the unifromMat4 method is called on the shader object passing in the name of the uniform as a String ("model") and the matrix as an argument. This will update the value of the model uniform in the shader to the value contained in the mat variable.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the location of a uniform in a GPU program using the `GL20` class and method `glGetUniformLocation`.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location to retrieve in the program's uniform buffer.

returns.description: an integer representing the location of a uniform in a GPU program.

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: loads a shader program from a file and validates it. It creates a new shader object and returns it upon successful loading.

params.name: filename
params.description: path to a file containing the vertex and fragment shaders to be loaded into the program.

returns.description:
a `Shader` object representing a compiled shader program.

* `Shader`: This is the type of the returned object, which is a `Shader` instance representing a shader program created by combining a vertex shader and a fragment shader.
* `program`: This is the handle of the created shader program, which can be used for further operations such as attaching the shader to a GPU context or linking it with other shaders.

The function takes two string arguments: `filename` and `GL20.GL_VERTEX_SHADER` (or `GL20.GL_FRAGMENT_SHADER`). The first argument is the filename of the shader file to be loaded, while the second argument specifies which type of shader to load from the file (either vertex or fragment shader).

The function creates a new shader program using the `glCreateProgram` function of the OpenGL API, and then loads the specified shader from the file using `glCreateShader` functions. Once the shaders are loaded, the function validates the program by calling the `validateProgram` function, and returns a new `Shader` instance representing the combined shader program.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates a new shader object and loads shader source code from a string parameter. It compiles the shader and attaches it to a program object if compilation succeeds. If compilation fails, an error message is printed and the program exits.

params.name: target
params.description: type of shader being created, which determines the shader's functionality and usage in the program.

params.name: src
params.description: Shader source code to be compiled and linked with the specified target and program.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program that the loaded shader will be attached to and used by.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a program object by calling `glLinkProgram`, `glValidateProgram`, and checking their return values. If any of these calls fail, it prints an error message and exits the program with a non-zero status code.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program to be validated, which is linked and then validated using the `glLinkProgram()` and `glValidateProgram()` functions respectively.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads the contents of a specified file and returns a String containing its characters.

params.name: file
params.description: path of a file that contains text, which is read and returned as a string by the `getText()` function.

returns.description: a string representation of the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText

structured.description: a class `Model` that represents a 3D model in an OpenGL context. It provides a constructor to set the vertex array object (VAO) and the number of vertices, and a method `draw()` to render the model. The `enableAttribs()` and `disableAttribs()` methods enable and disable specific vertex attributes, respectively. The `getVAO()` and `getSize()` methods provide accessors for the VAO and size, respectively. Additionally, the code provides a factory method `load()` to load a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices.

items.description: is used to handle vertex and index buffer data for rendering 3D models in OpenGL. It provides methods for binding and unbinding Vertex Array Objects (VAOs) and Element Array Buffers (EABs), as well as storing vertex and index data and enabling/disabling vertex attributtes. The class also provides a way to load pre-defined model data from an array of vertices and indices.
items.name: Model

items.description: binds a vertex array object, enables vertex attributes for position and texture coord, draws triangles using `glDrawElements`, disables vertex attributes, and unbinds the vertex array object.
items.name: draw

items.description: enables two vertex attributes in a GL context using `glEnableVertexAttribArray`.
items.name: enableAttribs

items.description: disables both vertex attribute arrays for a specific GL context.
items.name: disableAttribs

items.description: returns the value of the variable `vao`.

returns.description: an integer value representing the VAO.

usage.code:
Model model = Model.load(vertices, indices);
model.getVAO(); // returns the VAO value

items.name: getVAO

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `size`.
items.name: getSize

items.description: loads a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices. It creates a Vertex Array Object (VAO), stores the indices, and then binds and unbinds the VAO. Finally, it returns a `Model` object representing the loaded model.

params.name: vertices
params.description:
3D geometric data of the model to be loaded, which is stored in an array of floating-point values.

* `vertices`: An array of floating-point values representing the 3D vertices of a model.
* `indices`: An array of integers representing the indices of the vertices in the model.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D model's vertex indices, which are used to bind the vertices of the model to its corresponding geometry in the graphics pipeline.

* `indices`: An `int[]` array containing the indices of the vertices in the model.
* `v_count`: The number of vertices in the model, which can be calculated by summing up the lengths of all the `indices` arrays.

returns.description:
a `Model` object representing the loaded 3D model.

* `Model`: This is the type of the output, which is an instance of the `Model` class.
* `vao`: This is an integer variable representing the VAO (Vertex Array Object) handle created by the function.
* `v_count`: This is an integer variable representing the number of vertices in the model.

items.name: load

items.description: generates a new vertex array object (Vao) and binds it to the current context, allowing for its use in rendering graphics.

returns.description: an integer value representing the unique vertex array object (VAO) created.

items.name: createVAO

items.description: stores floating-point array data into a buffer object and sets vertex attributes for rendering.

params.name: attrib
params.description: 3D vertex attribute index that stores the data for the current buffer in the GPU's memory.

params.name: data
params.description:
2D array of floating-point values that will be stored in the vertex buffer object (VBO).

* `data`: An array of 4 float values representing vertex data.
* `attrib`: An integer representing the attribute index to which the vertex data will be mapped. There are two attribute indices in total, assigned using the `GL20.glVertexAttribPointer()` method.
* `vbo`: A buffer object handle generated by `GL15.glGenBuffers()`. The buffer is bound using `GL15.glBindBuffer(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, vbo)` and data is transferred to it using `GL15.glBufferData(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, Util.createFlippedBuffer(data), GL15.GL_STATIC_DRAW)`.

items.name: storeData

items.description: genrates a new buffer object and binds it to an element array buffer, then stores the provided integer array data in the buffer using the `glBufferData` method.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D indices of the vertices of a geometric shape, which are stored in a buffer for later use in rendering.

* `indices` is an integer array representing a buffer containing indices for an element array buffer.
* The length of the array is specified by the size of the `indices` array, which is a variable passed as a parameter to the function.
* Each element in the `indices` array represents an index into a buffer for an element array, with each index being an integer value between 0 and the total number of elements in the buffer - 1, inclusive.

items.name: storeIndices

items.description: binds a vertex array object (VAO) using the `GL30.glBindVertexArray()` method, setting it to null.
items.name: unbindVAO
